name: AfroTools AI Product Architecture

frameworkDecision.decision: Use vanilla browser JavaScript modules, static HTML, shared data artifacts, and Netlify functions instead of adding a SPA framework.
frameworkDecision.reason: AfroTools already ships a large static-first catalog. The AI layer should route, prefill, explain, and export through existing tools without making every page depend on a heavy client runtime.

principles: Existing AfroTools tools stay canonical; AI selects and prepares them rather than replacing calculators or workflows.
Deterministic routing and full-catalog retrieval run before optional model calls.
Primary Ask surfaces expose refinement controls for missing details and related existing tools without adding raw prompts to URLs.
Provider calls are consented, schema-validated, prompt-governed, and allowed to choose only known existing_tool_call records.
Partner widgets may render inside iframe embeds, but their AfroTools handoff links must open top-level AfroTools routes in a new browsing context.
Sensitive workflow content stays browser-local by default and must not be stored in analytics, URLs, eval reports, or model training artifacts.
Model readiness is proven by generated synthetic corpus, held-out splits, eval gates, drift intake, and operator dashboards.

answerEngine.goal: Turn Ask AfroTools AI from a generic tool finder into a search-style answer engine that extracts obvious entities, shows what it understood, and then launches the existing calculator or workflow.
runtimeOrder: local entity extraction for country, amount, task, document type, route, and missing inputs
answer preview composer with source, confidence, and no unsupported calculation claims
sessionStorage prefill handoff into canonical tools
optional consented model explanation after the user chooses AI help
answerEngine.currentPilot: Angola salary-tax prompts extract Angola, typo variant Angoa, gross pay, pay period, AOA/Kz context, and route to /angola/ao-paye without placing salary values in the URL.

tokenStrategy.policy: Send only the top catalog chunk, structured extracted inputs, source labels, and tool-call schema to a provider. Never send the full public catalog or raw sensitive user content by default.
compression: compact tool records to id, route, title, category, sourcePolicy, safetyDomain, required inputs, and hints
bucket prompt length in analytics instead of storing raw prompts
summarize previous workflow state into sanitized structured inputs before any consented model call

forbiddenSources: emails, phone numbers, account identifiers, or private profile data
salary, legal, health, financial, or education-profile details unless converted into sanitized non-identifying fixtures
trainingDataStrategy.evaluation: Promote routing, prompt, or fine-tune changes only after held-out tool-call evals, privacy guardrail tests, and browser workflow checks pass.

modelPath.description: Use deterministic extraction, retrieval, and answer previews for obvious user tasks with zero provider cost.
Use the existing provider port only when the deterministic result is ambiguous and the user consents.
Fine-tune or distill a small tool-call router on sanitized synthetic and reviewed examples, not on private user text.

Train AfroTools-owned domain models for routing and explanation style after corpus scale, eval quality, cost, and privacy controls are proven. This is the realistic path toward DeepSeek-like capability, not a first release dependency.

layers.label: User and partner interfaces
layers.responsibility: Collect tasks and display existing-tool handoffs without embedding the tools in iframes.

layers.label: Routing and retrieval
layers.responsibility: Map natural-language tasks to known routes across the full router-safe manifest.

layers.label: Existing-tool invocation
layers.responsibility: Turn a router decision into launchable route-only or session-prefill execution plans.

layers.label: Model provider ports
layers.responsibility: Constrain optional model routing and generation behind prompt registry, provider abstraction, and response validation.

layers.label: Model operations
layers.responsibility: Generate training/eval artifacts, readiness gates, drift queues, and operator views.

layers.label: Safety, privacy, and telemetry
layers.responsibility: Block unsafe decisions, avoid raw prompt persistence, and record metadata-only feedback for drift review.

mermaid:
flowchart TD
  U[Homepage / AI / Ask / Search / Widget / API] --> A[AI orchestrator]
  A --> R[Deterministic router + full catalog retrieval]
  R --> C[Generated tool catalog pack]
  R --> T[Existing AfroTools tool call]
  T --> X[Invocation runtime + prefill adapters]
  X --> E[Canonical AfroTools tools]
  A -->|consent + not obvious| P[Prompt registry + provider port]
  P --> V[Schema + guardrail validation]
  V --> T
  O[Corpus + splits + evals + drift intake] --> L[Router Lab + readiness report]
  L --> A
